items.title: A Case of Vibrio vulnificus Infection in a Patient with an Underlying Condition after Consuming Frozen Seafood
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: To prevent vibriosis, frozen seafood should be thoroughly heat treatment before consumption. Proper hygiene practices, such as washing hands after handling raw seafood and storing raw seafood separately from other foods, are also essential. High-risk individuals, particularly those with underlying conditions such as chronic liver disease, should strictly adhere to these prevention guidelines to reduce the risk of infection.
items.conclusion: To prevent vibriosis, frozen seafood should be thoroughly heat treatment before consumption. Proper hygiene practices, such as washing hands after handling raw seafood and storing raw seafood separately from other foods, are also essential

Machine-learned potentials (MLPs) for large molecules and clusters offer a viable alternative to obtaining the quartic force field (QFF). items.title: Supply Chain Events and Risk of Drug Shortage in Canada
items.description: CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE: In this study of shortage incidence following Canadian supply chain events, 9 in 10 reported supply chain events did not experience true shortages. These findings highlight a need for a unified shortage definition focusing on real disruptions in supply and can be used to guide policies to manage drug shortages, improve patient outcomes, and enhance health care delivery.

items.title: Smartphone-Based Proactive Self-Screening for Ocular Surface Malignancies: A Nonrandomized Clinical Trial
items.description: CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE: This trial found that the integration of smartphone-enabled imaging, AI-driven diagnostics, and targeted media outreach established a scalable, accessible, and potentially clinically effective strategy for population-level screening of rare ocular malignancies. This closed-loop mobile health model potentially addresses gaps in early detection and equitable care delivery for vision- and life-threatening rare diseases.
items.conclusion: AND RELEVANCE: This trial found that the integration of smartphone-enabled imaging, AI-driven diagnostics, and targeted media outreach established a scalable, accessible, and potentially clinically effective strategy for population-level screening of rare ocular malignancies. items.title: PD-(L)1 Inhibitor Monotherapy vs Chemoimmunotherapy for Advanced NSCLC With High PD-L1 Expression: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
items.description: CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE: In this meta-analysis of phase 3 RCTs, chemoimmunotherapy was associated with significantly improved OS and PFS compared with PD-(L)1 inhibitor monotherapy in patients with advanced NSCLC and high PD-L1 expression. Prospective trials are needed to confirm these findings.
items.conclusion: AND RELEVANCE: In this meta-analysis of phase 3 RCTs, chemoimmunotherapy was associated with significantly improved OS and PFS compared with PD-(L)1 inhibitor monotherapy in patients with advanced NSCLC and high PD-L1 expression. Prospective trials are needed to confirm these findings

items.title: Very Preterm Infant Retinal Microanatomy at 36 Weeks' Postmenstrual Age and 2-Year Neurodevelopment
items.description: CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE: In this cohort study, RNFL thickness in VPT infants at 36 weeks' PMA predicted motor and cognitive neurodevelopmental outcomes at age 2 years. These findings, if confirmed independently, could help guide management of VPT infants through age 2 years.

items.description: Interface engineering using multifunctional materials is central to advancing both efficiency and operational stability in perovskite solar cells (PSCs). items.title: HDAC inhibition reprograms stem cell fate to suppress infantile hemangioma vasculogenesis
items.description: Infantile hemangioma (IH), the most common vascular tumor of infancy, relies on hemangioma stem cells (HemSCs) to drive pathological vasculogenesis during the proliferating phase. items.title: Phase-resolved functional lung magnetic resonance imaging for evaluation of lung perfusion and ventilation in fibrosing interstitial lung diseases
items.description: CONCLUSION: PREFUL MRI provides a quantitative functional evaluation of ventilation and perfusion in F-ILD patients, demonstrating strong correlations with pulmonary function parameters and fibrotic lesions. It shows potential as a valuable monitoring tool enabling severity assessment of F-ILD.

items.description: Friedreich's ataxia (FA) is a progressive autosomal recessive neurodegenerative disorder caused by frataxin (FXN) deficiency, resulting in mitochondrial dysfunction, oxidative stress, defective autophagy, and progressive motor impairment. Despite extensive efforts, effective disease-modifying therapies for FA remain lacking. items.title: Fifteen years of intrathecal baclofen therapy in China: complications, safety governance, and a multimodal neuromodulation strategy
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: This study presents one of the largest reported ITB cohort in China. items.title: Efficacy of the optilume® drug-coated balloon for recurrent urethral stricture after prior urethroplasty: a 12-month multicenter follow-up study
items.description: CONCLUSION: The Optilume® DCB represents an effective and minimally invasive salvage treatment for recurrent anterior urethral stricture after urethroplasty, providing durable functional improvement and low retreatment rates at 12 months.

items.description: Heart failure (HF) remains a leading cause of morbidity and mortality worldwide, with particularly poor outcomes in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). items.title: Soft fibrin matrix instructs macrophage M2 polarization via FABP4-mediated enhancement of fatty acid β-oxidation to alleviate MASH
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: Our findings reveal that soft fibrin matrices promote anti-inflammatory macrophage polarization and mitigate MASH by enhancing fatty acid β-oxidation via downregulation of FABP4 and stabilization of PPARα. items.description: Ochratoxin A (OTA) poses significant health risks and is prevalent in various plant-based foods, necessitating rapid and reliable on-site detection methods. items.description: Umbilical cord blood HSCs transplantation is limited by low cell numbers and engraftment efficiency. The bone marrow endothelial cell (EC) niche is vital for HSCs. items.title: Association of obstructive airway disease with COMISA: findings from the Turkish sleep apnea database (TURKAPNE) cohort
items.description: CONCLUSIONS: In this nationwide sleep-clinic cohort, OAD was independently associated with COMISA. COMISA patients with OAD exhibited more adverse clinical and polysomnographic features. items.description: We evaluate an intervention designed to give lab-based research teams an opportunity to intentionally discuss project-related data management practices within their labs, examining how such communication might inform perceptions of the relationship between formal data management plans and lab members' day-to-day data management practices. items.description: Aging research has made remarkable progress in describing aging through the genetic architecture of longevity, epigenetic clocks, proteomic signatures, and systems-level analyses. items.title: Neoagarotetraose from Marine Red Algae Ameliorates UVB-Induced Skin Barrier Damage in Mice and HaCaT Keratinocytes
items.description: Ultraviolet B (UVB) radiation is a major environmental threat that disrupts skin integrity and drives inflammatory skin disorders. Neoagarotetraose (NA4), a low-molecular-weight oligosaccharide derived from marine red algae, is characterized by high bioavailability and well-documented anti-inflammatory properties, yet its role in UVB-induced skin damage remains unexplored. items.description: The assessment of cystic fibrosis transmembrane conductance regulator (CFTR) activity is essential to both diagnose and evaluate the efficacy of treatments in cystic fibrosis (CF). items.description: The pervasive global dispersion of organophosphorus pesticides (OPs) as a primary insecticidal agent for enhanced agricultural productivity presents an enduring ecological and human health exigency. items.title: Post-marketing safety profile of palivizumab: a 20-year pharmacovigilance study using the FDA Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS)
items.description: Palivizumab is an FDA-approved monoclonal antibody for preventing severe respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) infections in high-risk children.
